gitState.notes: Workspace has pre-existing untracked .pan/prd.md plus planning artifacts created by this session. No implementation files were modified during planning.

decisions.summary: Auto-planning mode is authoritative: do not ask user questions; infer defensible defaults and record them in the vBRIEF autoDecisions list.
Make Home the canonical dashboard landing route at /, keep Pipeline reachable at /pipeline, and update sidebar/logo navigation so existing users retain an explicit Pipeline destination.

Build Home from existing event-sourced dashboard state and PAN-1052 memory surfaces: Zustand selectors, MemoryObservation/MemoryStatus, WorkspaceStatusCard, and time-bucket helpers where they already fit.

Compose briefing refresh and compliance warning through the existing memory hook path instead of adding a disconnected hook chain: the UserPromptSubmit path already posts to /api/memory/inject and writes injected context to stdout.

Store the feature registry at ~/.panopticon/registry/features.sqlite, but keep dashboard-server access asynchronous via a worker or async service boundary so route handlers never block the Node event loop.
Ship compliance in advisory mode by default: trigger detection and misses are logged and surfaced, but v1 must not block tool calls or enforce behavior.
Use one shared briefing assembler for the dashboard writer, pan briefing, and hook reinjection so terminal and UI sessions receive the same live context text.
The briefing must honestly frame cross-workspace state: do not inline all sibling memory; point agents to pan memory search --all-workspaces and registry ownership records for follow-up.
Browser verification for the Home tab must use an isolated Playwright/browser profile and not depend on another agent's browser state.

hazards.summary: Dashboard server code must not add execSync/readFileSync/writeFileSync/readdirSync/statSync or direct blocking SQLite work in route/service paths.
hazards.mitigation: Use fs/promises, Effect services, execAsync/spawn, and a worker/service boundary for registry SQLite access; review must block any synchronous server-reachable calls.

hazards.summary: Changing / from Pipeline to Home can break implicit operator muscle memory and tests that assume Pipeline renders at root.
hazards.mitigation: Add /pipeline as the explicit Pipeline route, update nav/logo behavior, and include route tests for /, /pipeline, /board, and /conv/* compatibility.

hazards.summary: UserPromptSubmit hook stdout composition is fragile because multiple injections may need to prepend/append prompt content.
hazards.mitigation: Integrate briefing refresh and advisory warning inside the existing memory injection response path with deterministic ordering: warning first, original prompt, then fresh briefing/memory context blocks as applicable.

hazards.summary: Briefing freshness reinjection can spam the prompt if freshness markers are not tracked per session and per briefing mtime.
hazards.mitigation: Record session start and last-injected briefing mtime under ~/.panopticon/sessions/<sessionId>/ and only reinject after the file mtime advances beyond the last injected value.

hazards.summary: Registry classification introduces LLM cost and model-routing risk during issue creation/state updates.
hazards.mitigation: Keep classification async/non-blocking, low-cost, configurable, and overrideable through pan registry tag; tests should mock the classifier.

hazards.summary: Compliance audit must not become a destructive or blocking enforcement mechanism in this issue.
hazards.mitigation: Default config to advisory, implement enforcing mode only as a reported unsupported/future value if present, and verify misses never prevent subsequent tool use.

hazards.summary: Home tab data sources may be empty on fresh installs or when PAN-1052 memory has not yet produced observations/status rollups.
hazards.mitigation: Render clear empty states for summary, activity feed, workspace cards, and registry rather than treating missing data as an error.

hazards.summary: Claude Code JSONL session files are sacred and must never be deleted, truncated, or treated as cleanup artifacts while implementing hooks or session freshness markers.
hazards.mitigation: Store new briefing freshness markers under Panopticon session state paths only; never mutate ~/.claude/projects/*.jsonl.

hazards.summary: Dashboard server must run under Node 22 built dist, not Bun or tsx source-mode, when manually verifying server behavior.
hazards.mitigation: After dashboard server changes, run npm run build before restart and use pan up / Node 22 dist server for verification.

hazards.summary: Compliance miss logging and registry APIs must not introduce speculative destructive HTTP requests or deep-wipe calls.
hazards.mitigation: Restrict implementation to local files/SQLite/memory observations and safe GET/POST endpoints that do not destroy workspaces, branches, tmux sessions, or JSONL history.

sessionHistory.content: Planning session for PAN-1204 began from the issue orchestration context and PRD. Discovery found the current dashboard default route maps / to pipeline in src/dashboard/frontend/src/App.tsx; the Tab union lives in Header.tsx; Sidebar.tsx controls nav groups and logo behavior; WorkspaceStatusCard, useObservationFeed, timeBuckets, and Zustand selectors provide reusable Home data surfaces; PAN-1052 memory contracts and hook routes already support observation/status data and prompt-time injection; setup/hooks.ts and caveman/workspace.ts already register Stop, SessionStart, and UserPromptSubmit hook paths. No implementation files were modified during planning.
sessionHistory.note: Planning artifacts created for PAN-1204: Home tab + live session-context briefing with advisory compliance audit hook
